info.name: Exodus
info.desc: The Second Book of Moses called Exodus

1: The sons of Jacob who went to Egypt with him, each with his family, were
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
2: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah,
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
3: Issachar, Zebulun, Benjamin,
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
4: Dan, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
5:
The total number of these people directly descended from Jacob was seventy.
 His son Joseph was already in Egypt.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
1.5 One ancient translation 75 (see Acts 7.14).
6: In the course of time Joseph, his brothers, and all the rest of that generation died,
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
7: but their descendants, the Israelites, had many children and became so numerous and strong that Egypt was filled with them.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
Acts 7.17
8: Then, a new king, who knew nothing about Joseph, came to power in Egypt.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
Acts 7.18
9: He said to his people, “These Israelites are so numerous and strong that they are a threat to us.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
10:
In case of war they might join our enemies in order to fight against us, and might escape from
 the country. We must find some way to keep them from becoming even more numerous.”
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
Acts 7.19; 1.10 escape from; or take control of.
11: So the Egyptians put slave-drivers over them to crush their spirits with hard labour. The Israelites built the cities of Pithom and Rameses to serve as supply centres for the king.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
12: But the more the Egyptians oppressed the Israelites, the more they increased in number and the further they spread through the land. The Egyptians came to fear the Israelites
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
13-14: and made their lives miserable by forcing them into cruel slavery. They made them work on their building projects and in their fields, and they had no mercy on them.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
15: Then the king of Egypt spoke to Shiphrah and Puah, the two midwives who helped the Hebrew women.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
16: “When you help the Hebrew women give birth,” he said to them, “kill the baby if it is a boy; but if it is a girl, let it live.”
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
17: But the midwives feared God and so did not obey the king; instead, they let the boys live.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
18: So the king sent for the midwives and asked them, “Why are you doing this? Why are you letting the boys live?”
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
19: They answered, “The Hebrew women are not like Egyptian women; they give birth easily, and their babies are born before either of us gets there.”
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
20-21: Because the midwives feared God, he was good to them and gave them families of their own. And the Israelites continued to increase and become strong.
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
22: Finally the king issued a command to all his people: “Take every newborn Hebrew boy and throw him into the Nile, but let all the girls live.”
The Israelites are Treated Cruelly in Egypt
Acts 7.19

1: During this time a man from the tribe of Levi married a woman of his own tribe,
2: and she bore him a son. When she saw what a fine baby he was, she hid him for three months.
The Birth of Moses
Acts 7.20; Heb 11.23
3: But when she could not hide him any longer, she took a basket made of reeds and covered it with tar to make it watertight. She put the baby in it and then placed it in the tall grass at the edge of the river.
The Birth of Moses
4: The baby's sister stood some distance away to see what would happen to him.
The Birth of Moses
5: The king's daughter came down to the river to bathe, while her servants walked along the bank. Suddenly she noticed the basket in the tall grass and sent a slave woman to get it.
The Birth of Moses
6: The princess opened it and saw a baby boy. He was crying, and she felt sorry for him. “This is one of the Hebrew babies,” she said.
The Birth of Moses
7: Then his sister asked her, “Shall I go and call a Hebrew woman to act as a wet nurse?”
The Birth of Moses
8: “Please do,” she answered. So the girl went and brought the baby's own mother.
The Birth of Moses
9: The princess told the woman, “Take this baby and nurse him for me, and I will pay you.” So she took the baby and nursed him.
The Birth of Moses
10: Later, when the child was old enough, she took him to the king's daughter, who adopted him as her own son. She said to herself, “I pulled him out of the water, and so I name him Moses.”
Moses Escapes to Midian
2.10 Moses: This name sounds like the Hebrew for “pull out”.; Acts 7.21
11: When Moses had grown up, he went out to visit his people, the Hebrews, and he saw how they were forced to do hard labour. He even saw an Egyptian kill a Hebrew, one of Moses' own people.
Moses Escapes to Midian
12: Moses looked all round, and when he saw that no one was watching, he killed the Egyptian and hid his body in the sand.
Moses Escapes to Midian
13: The next day he went back and saw two Hebrew men fighting. He said to the one who was in the wrong, “Why are you beating up a fellow-Hebrew?”
14: The man answered, “Who made you our ruler and judge? Are you going to kill me just as you killed that Egyptian?” Then Moses was afraid and said to himself, “People have found out what I have done.”
Moses Escapes to Midian
15-16:
When the king heard about what had happened, he tried to have Moses killed, but Moses fled and went to live in the land of Midian.
One day, when Moses was sitting by a well, seven daughters of Jethro, the priest of Midian, came to draw water and fill the troughs for their father's sheep and goats.
17: But some shepherds drove Jethro's daughters away. Then Moses went to their rescue and watered their animals for them.
Moses Escapes to Midian
18: When they returned to their father, he asked, “Why have you come back so early today?”
Moses Escapes to Midian
19: “An Egyptian rescued us from the shepherds,” they answered, “and he even drew water for us and watered our animals.”
Moses Escapes to Midian
20: “Where is he?” he asked his daughters. “Why did you leave the man out there? Go and invite him to eat with us.”
Moses Escapes to Midian
21: So Moses agreed to live there, and Jethro gave him his daughter Zipporah in marriage,
22: who bore him a son. Moses said to himself, “I am a foreigner in this land, and so I name him Gershom.”
Moses Escapes to Midian
2.22 Gershom: This name sounds like the Hebrew for “foreigner”.
23: Years later the king of Egypt died, but the Israelites were still groaning under their slavery and cried out for help. Their cry went up to God,
Moses Escapes to Midian
24: who heard their groaning and remembered his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
25: He saw the slavery of the Israelites and was concerned for them.
Moses Escapes to Midian
2.25 was concerned for them; one ancient translation revealed himself to them.

1: One day while Moses was taking care of the sheep and goats of his father-in-law Jethro, the priest of Midian, he led the flock across the desert and came to Sinai, the holy mountain.
2:
There the angel of the 
LORD
 appeared to him as a flame coming from the middle of a bush. Moses saw that the bush was on fire but that it was not burning up.
God Calls Moses
3: “This is strange,” he thought. “Why isn't the bush burning up? I will go closer and see.”
God Calls Moses
4:
When the 
LORD
 saw that Moses was coming closer, he called to him from the middle of the bush and said, “Moses! Moses!”
He answered, “Yes, here I am.”
God Calls Moses
5: God said, “Do not come any closer. Take off your sandals, because you are standing on holy ground.
God Calls Moses
6: I am the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” So Moses covered his face, because he was afraid to look at God.
God Calls Moses
7:
Then the 
LORD
 said, “I have seen how cruelly my people are being treated in Egypt; I have heard them cry out to be rescued from their slave-drivers. I know all about their sufferings,
God Calls Moses
8: and so I have come down to rescue them from the Egyptians and to bring them out of Egypt to a spacious land, one which is rich and fertile and in which the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites now live.
God Calls Moses
9: I have indeed heard the cry of my people, and I see how the Egyptians are oppressing them.
God Calls Moses
10: Now I am sending you to the king of Egypt so that you can lead my people out of his country.”
God Calls Moses
11: But Moses said to God, “I am nobody. How can I go to the king and bring the Israelites out of Egypt?”
God Calls Moses
12: God answered, “I will be with you, and when you bring the people out of Egypt, you will worship me on this mountain. That will be the proof that I have sent you.”
God Calls Moses
13: But Moses replied, “When I go to the Israelites and say to them, ‘The God of your ancestors sent me to you,’ they will ask me, ‘What is his name?’ So what can I tell them?”
14:
God said, “I am who I am. This is what you must say to them: ‘The one who is called I AM
 has sent me to you.’
God Calls Moses

3.14 I am who I am… I AM; or I will be who I will be… I WILL BE. “I am” sounds like the Hebrew name Yahweh, traditionally transliterated as Jehovah. This name is represented in this translation by “the LORD” in capital letters, following a usage which is widespread in English versions.; Rev 1.4, 8
15:
Tell the Israelites that I, the 
LORD
, the God of their ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, have sent you to them. This is my name for ever; this is what all future generations are to call me.
God Calls Moses
16:
Go and gather the leaders of Israel together and tell them that I, the 
LORD
, the God of their ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, appeared to you. Tell them that I have come to them and have seen what the Egyptians are doing to them.
God Calls Moses
17: I have decided that I will bring them out of Egypt, where they are being treated cruelly, and will take them to a rich and fertile land — the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites.
God Calls Moses
18:
“My people will listen to what you say to them. Then you must go with the leaders of Israel to the king of Egypt and say to him, ‘The 
LORD
, the God of the Hebrews, has revealed himself to us. Now allow us to travel for three days into the desert to offer sacrifices to the 
LORD
, our God.’
God Calls Moses
19: I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go unless he is forced to do so.
God Calls Moses
20: But I will use my power and will punish Egypt by doing terrifying things there. After that he will let you go.
God Calls Moses
21: “I will make the Egyptians respect you so that when my people leave, they will not go empty-handed.
22: Every Israelite woman will go to her Egyptian neighbours and to any Egyptian woman living in her house and will ask for clothing and for gold and silver jewellery. The Israelites will put these things on their sons and daughters and carry away the wealth of the Egyptians.”
God Calls Moses

1:
Then Moses answered the 
LORD
, “But suppose the Israelites do not believe me and will not listen to what I say. What shall I do if they say that you did not appear to me?”
2:
So the 
LORD
 asked him, “What are you holding?”
“A stick,” he answered.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
3:
The 
LORD
 said, “Throw it on the ground.” When Moses threw it down, it turned into a snake, and he ran away from it.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
4:
Then the 
LORD
 said to Moses, “Bend down and pick it up by the tail.” So Moses bent down and caught it, and it became a stick again.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
5:
The 
LORD
 said, “Do this to prove to the Israelites that the 
LORD
, the God of their ancestors, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, has appeared to you.”
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
6:
The 
LORD
 spoke to Moses again, “Put your hand inside your robe.” Moses obeyed; and when he took his hand out, it was diseased, covered with white spots, like snow.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
7:
Then the 
LORD
 said, “Put your hand inside your robe again.” He did so, and when he took it out this time, it was healthy, just like the rest of his body.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
8:
The 
LORD
 said, “If they will not believe you or be convinced by the first miracle, then this one will convince them.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
9: If in spite of these two miracles they still will not believe you, and if they refuse to listen to what you say, take some water from the Nile and pour it on the ground. The water will turn into blood.”
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
10:
But Moses said, “No, 
LORD
, don't send me. I have never been a good speaker, and I haven't become one since you began to speak to me. I am a poor speaker, slow and hesitant.”
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
11:
The 
LORD
 said to him, “Who gives man his mouth? Who makes him deaf or dumb? Who gives him sight or makes him blind? It is I, the 
LORD
.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
12: Now, go! I will help you to speak, and I will tell you what to say.”
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
13: But Moses answered, “No, Lord, please send someone else.”
14:
At this the 
LORD
 became angry with Moses and said, “What about your brother Aaron, the Levite? I know that he can speak well. In fact, he is now coming to meet you and will be glad to see you.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
15: You can speak to him and tell him what to say. I will help both of you to speak, and I will tell you both what to do.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
16: He will be your spokesman and speak to the people for you. Then you will be like God, telling him what to say.
God Gives Moses Miraculous Power
17: Take this stick with you; for with it you will perform miracles.”
Moses Returns to Egypt
18: Then Moses went back to Jethro, his father-in-law, and said to him, “Please let me go back to my relatives in Egypt to see if they are still alive.” Jethro agreed and said goodbye to him.
Moses Returns to Egypt
19:
While Moses was still in Midian, the 
LORD
 said to him, “Go back to Egypt, for all those who wanted to kill you are dead.”
Moses Returns to Egypt
20: So Moses took his wife and his sons, put them on a donkey, and set out with them for Egypt, carrying the stick that God had told him to take.
Moses Returns to Egypt
21:
Again the 
LORD
 said to Moses, “Now that you are going back to Egypt, be sure to perform before the king all the miracles which I have given you the power to do. But I will make the king stubborn, and he will not let the people go.
22:
Then you must tell him that I, the 
LORD
, say, ‘Israel is my firstborn son.
Moses Returns to Egypt
23: I told you to let my son go, so that he might worship me, but you refused. Now I am going to kill your firstborn son.’ ”
Moses Returns to Egypt
24:
At a camping place on the way to Egypt, the 
LORD
 met Moses and tried to kill him.
25-26:
Then Zipporah, his wife, took a sharp stone, cut off the foreskin of her son, and touched Moses' feet
 with it. Because of the rite of circumcision she said to Moses, “You are a husband of blood to me.” And so the 
LORD
 spared Moses' life.
; 4.25–26 feet: This reference to “feet” is thought by some to be a euphemism for the genitals.
27:
Meanwhile the 
LORD
 had said to Aaron, “Go into the desert to meet Moses.” So he went to meet him at the holy mountain; and when he met him, he kissed him.
Moses Returns to Egypt
28:
Then Moses told Aaron everything that the 
LORD
 had said when he told him to return to Egypt; he also told him about the miracles which the 
LORD
 had ordered him to perform.
Moses Returns to Egypt
29: So Moses and Aaron went to Egypt and gathered all the Israelite leaders together.
Moses Returns to Egypt
30:
Aaron told them everything that the 
LORD
 had said to Moses, and then Moses performed all the miracles in front of the people.
Moses Returns to Egypt
31:
They believed, and when they heard that the 
LORD
 had come to them and had seen how they were being treated cruelly, they bowed down and worshipped.
Moses Returns to Egypt

1:
Then Moses and Aaron went to the king of Egypt and said, “The 
LORD
, the God of Israel, says, ‘Let my people go, so that they can hold a festival in the desert to honour me.’ ”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
2:
“Who is the 
LORD
?” the king demanded. “Why should I listen to him and let Israel go? I do not know the 
LORD
; and I will not let Israel go.”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
3:
Moses and Aaron replied, “The God of the Hebrews has revealed himself to us. Allow us to travel for three days into the desert to offer sacrifices to the 
LORD
 our God. If we don't do so, he will kill us with disease or by war.”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
4: The king said to Moses and Aaron, “What do you mean by making the people neglect their work? Get those slaves back to work!
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
5: You people have become more numerous than the Egyptians. And now you want to stop working!”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
6: That same day the king commanded the Egyptian slave-drivers and the Israelite foremen:
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
7: “Stop giving the people straw for making bricks. Make them go and find it for themselves.
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
8: But still require them to make the same number of bricks as before, not one brick less. They haven't enough work to do, and that is why they keep asking me to let them go and offer sacrifices to their God!
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
9: Make these people work harder and keep them busy, so that they won't have time to listen to a pack of lies.”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
10: The slave-drivers and the Israelite foremen went out and said to the Israelites, “The king has said that he will not supply you with any more straw.
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
11: He says that you must go and get it for yourselves wherever you can find it, but you must still make the same number of bricks.”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
12: So the people went all over Egypt looking for straw.
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
13: The slave-drivers kept trying to force them to make the same number of bricks every day as they had made when they were given straw.
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
14: The Egyptian slave-drivers beat the Israelite foremen, whom they had put in charge of the work. They demanded, “Why aren't you people making the same number of bricks as you made before?”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
15: Then the foremen went to the king and complained, “Why do you do this to us, Your Majesty?
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
16: We are given no straw, but we are still ordered to make bricks! And now we are being beaten. It is your people that are at fault.”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
17:
The king answered, “You are lazy and don't want to work, and that is why you ask me to let you go and offer sacrifices to the 
LORD
.
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
18: Now get back to work! You will not be given any straw, but you must still make the same number of bricks.”
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
19: The foremen realized that they were in trouble when they were told that they had to make the same number of bricks every day as they had made before.
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
20: As they were leaving, they met Moses and Aaron, who were waiting for them.
Moses and Aaron before the King of Egypt
21:
They said to Moses and Aaron, “The 
LORD
 has seen what you have done and will punish you for making the king and his officers hate us. You have given them an excuse to kill us.”
22:
Then Moses turned to the 
LORD
 again and said, “Lord, why do you ill-treat your people? Why did you send me here?
Moses Complains to the LORD
23: Ever since I went to the king to speak for you, he has treated them cruelly. And you have done nothing to help them!”
Moses Complains to the LORD

1:
Then the 
LORD
 said to Moses, “Now you are going to see what I will do to the king. I will force him to let my people go. In fact, I will force him to drive them out of his land.”
2:
God spoke to Moses and said, “I am the 
LORD
.
God Calls Moses
3:
I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob as Almighty God, but I did not make myself known to them by my holy name, the 
LORD
.
God Calls Moses
; 6.3 the LORD: See 3.14.
4: I also made my covenant with them, promising to give them the land of Canaan, the land in which they had lived as foreigners.
God Calls Moses
5: Now I have heard the groaning of the Israelites, whom the Egyptians have enslaved, and I have remembered my covenant.
God Calls Moses
6:
So tell the Israelites that I say to them, ‘I am the 
LORD
; I will rescue you and set you free from your slavery to the Egyptians. I will raise my mighty arm to bring terrible punishment upon them, and I will save you.
God Calls Moses
7:
I will make you my own people, and I will be your God. You will know that I am the 
LORD
 your God when I set you free from slavery in Egypt.
God Calls Moses
8:
I will bring you to the land that I solemnly promised to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob; and I will give it to you as your own possession. I am the 
LORD
.’ ”
God Calls Moses
9: Moses told this to the Israelites, but they would not listen to him, because their spirit had been broken by their cruel slavery.
God Calls Moses
10:
Then the 
LORD
 said to Moses,
God Calls Moses
11: “Go and tell the king of Egypt that he must let the Israelites leave his land.”
God Calls Moses
12: But Moses replied, “Even the Israelites will not listen to me, so why should the king? I am such a poor speaker.”
God Calls Moses
13:
The 
LORD
 commanded Moses and Aaron: “Tell the Israelites and the king of Egypt that I have ordered you to lead the Israelites out of Egypt.”
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
14: Reuben, Jacob's firstborn, had four sons: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi; they were the ancestors of the clans that bear their names.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
15: Simeon had six sons: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman; they were the ancestors of the clans that bear their names.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
16: Levi had three sons: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari; they were the ancestors of the clans that bear their names. Levi lived 137 years.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
17: Gershon had two sons: Libni and Shimei, and they had many descendants.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
18: Kohath had four sons: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel. Kohath lived 133 years.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
19: Merari had two sons: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of Levi with their descendants.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
20: Amram married his father's sister Jochebed, who bore him Aaron and Moses. Amram lived 137 years.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
21: Izhar had three sons: Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
22: Uzziel also had three sons: Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
23: Aaron married Elisheba, the daughter of Amminadab and sister of Nahshon; she bore him Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
24: Korah had three sons: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph; they were the ancestors of the divisions of the clan of Korah.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
25: Eleazar, Aaron's son, married one of Putiel's daughters, who bore him Phinehas. These were the heads of the families and the clans of the tribe of Levi.
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
26:
Aaron and Moses were the ones to whom the 
LORD
 said, “Lead the tribes of Israel out of Egypt.”
The Family Record of Moses and Aaron
27: They were the men who told the king of Egypt to free the Israelites.
The LORD's Command to Moses and Aaron
28:
When the 
LORD
 spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt,
The LORD's Command to Moses and Aaron
29:
he said, “I am the 
LORD
. Tell the king of Egypt everything I tell you.”
The LORD's Command to Moses and Aaron
30: But Moses answered, “You know that I am such a poor speaker; why should the king listen to me?”
The LORD's Command to Moses and Aaron

1:
The 
LORD
 said, “I am going to make you like God to the king, and your brother Aaron will speak to him as your prophet.
2: Tell Aaron everything I command you, and he will tell the king to let the Israelites leave his country.
3-4: But I will make the king stubborn, and he will not listen to you, no matter how many terrifying things I do in Egypt. Then I will bring severe punishment on Egypt and lead the tribes of my people out of the land.
5:
The Egyptians will then know that I am the 
LORD
, when I raise my hand against them and bring the Israelites out of their country.”
6:
Moses and Aaron did what the 
LORD
 commanded.
7: At the time when they spoke to the king, Moses was eighty years old, and Aaron was 83.
Aaron's Stick
8:
The 
LORD
 said to Moses and Aaron,
Aaron's Stick
9: “If the king demands that you prove yourselves by performing a miracle, tell Aaron to take his stick and throw it down in front of the king, and it will turn into a snake.”
Aaron's Stick
10:
So Moses and Aaron went to the king and did as the 
LORD
 had commanded. Aaron threw his stick down in front of the king and his officers, and it turned into a snake.
Aaron's Stick
11: Then the king called for his wise men and magicians, and by their magic they did the same thing.
Aaron's Stick
12: They threw down their sticks, and the sticks turned into snakes. But Aaron's stick swallowed theirs.
Aaron's Stick
13:
The king, however, remained stubborn and, just as the 
LORD
 had said, the king would not listen to Moses and Aaron.
14:
Then the 
LORD
 said to Moses, “The king is very stubborn and refuses to let the people go.
Disasters Strike Egypt
15: So go and meet him in the morning when he goes down to the Nile. Take with you the stick that was turned into a snake, and wait for him on the bank of the river.
Disasters Strike Egypt
16:
Then say to the king, ‘The 
LORD
, the God of the Hebrews, sent me to tell you to let his people go, so that they can worship him in the desert. But until now you have not listened.
Disasters Strike Egypt
17:
Now, Your Majesty, the 
LORD
 says that you will find out who he is by what he is going to do. Look, I am going to strike the surface of the river with this stick, and the water will be turned into blood.
Disasters Strike Egypt
18: The fish will die, and the river will stink so much that the Egyptians will not be able to drink from it.’ ”
Disasters Strike Egypt
19:
The 
LORD
 said to Moses, “Tell Aaron to take his stick and hold it out over all the rivers, canals, and pools in Egypt. The water will become blood, and all over the land there will be blood, even in the wooden tubs and stone jars.”
Disasters Strike Egypt
20:
Then Moses and Aaron did as the 
LORD
 commanded. In the presence of the king and his officers, Aaron raised his stick and struck the surface of the river, and all the water in it was turned into blood.
Disasters Strike Egypt
21: The fish in the river died, and it smelt so bad that the Egyptians could not drink from it. There was blood everywhere in Egypt.
22:
Then the king's magicians did the same thing by means of their magic, and the king was as stubborn as ever. Just as the 
LORD
 had said, the king refused to listen to Moses and Aaron.
Disasters Strike Egypt
23: Instead, he turned and went back to his palace without paying any attention even to this.
Disasters Strike Egypt
24: All the Egyptians dug along the bank of the river for drinking water, because they were not able to drink water from the river.
25:
Seven days passed after the 
LORD
 struck the river.
Disasters Strike Egypt

1:
Then the 
LORD
 said to Moses, “Go to the king and tell him that the 
LORD
 says, ‘Let my people go, so that they can worship me.
2: If you refuse, I will punish your country by covering it with frogs.

2: They made the ephod of fine linen; blue, purple, and red wool; and gold thread.
Making the Garments for the Priests
3: They hammered out sheets of gold and cut them into thin strips to be worked into the fine linen and into the blue, purple, and red wool.
Making the Garments for the Priests
4: They made two shoulder straps for the ephod and attached them to its sides, so that it could be fastened.
Making the Garments for the Priests
5:
The finely woven belt, made of the same materials, was attached to the ephod so as to form one piece with it, as the 
LORD
 had commanded Moses.
Making the Garments for the Priests
6: They prepared the carnelians and mounted them in gold settings; they were skilfully engraved with the names of the twelve sons of Jacob.
Making the Garments for the Priests
7:
They put them on the shoulder straps of the ephod to represent the twelve tribes of Israel, just as the 
LORD
 had commanded Moses.
Making the Breastpiece
8: They made the breastpiece of the same materials as the ephod and with similar embroidery.
Making the Breastpiece
9: It was square and folded double, 22 centimetres long and 22 centimetres wide.
Making the Breastpiece
10: They mounted four rows of precious stones on it: in the first row they mounted a ruby, a topaz, and a garnet;
Making the Breastpiece
11: in the second row, an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond;
Making the Breastpiece
12: in the third row, a turquoise, an agate, and an amethyst;
Making the Breastpiece
13: and in the fourth row, a beryl, a carnelian, and a jasper. These were mounted in gold settings.
14: Each of the twelve stones had engraved on it the name of one of the sons of Jacob, in order to represent the twelve tribes of Israel.
Making the Breastpiece
15: For the breastpiece they made chains of pure gold, twisted like cords.
Making the Breastpiece
16: They made two gold settings and two gold rings and attached the two rings to the upper corners of the breastpiece.
Making the Breastpiece
17: They fastened the two gold cords to the two rings
Making the Breastpiece

Making the Breastpiece
19: They made two rings of gold and attached them to the lower corners of the breastpiece, on the inside edge next to the ephod.
Making the Breastpiece
20: They made two more gold rings and attached them to the lower part of the front of the two shoulder straps of the ephod, near the seam and above the finely woven belt.
Making the Breastpiece
21:
Just as the 
LORD
 had commanded Moses, they tied the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod with a blue cord, so that the breastpiece rested above the belt and did not come loose.
